steps.title: Identify the food-finding motivation
steps.content: The underlined claim states that olms surface “to find food.” For evidence to support this, it must demonstrate that food available on the surface is actually consumed by olms.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the food-surfacing link
steps.content: If earthworms from surface soils are a major part of olms' diet, then olms must regularly access the surface to obtain them—directly supporting the claim that they surface to find food. The other findings address breeding frequency, habitat distribution, and brain structure, none of which explain the surface-food connection. That matches choice C — C is correct.
